articles.summary: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1, 2026. articles.title: CERT-EU: European Commission hack exposes data of 30 EU entities
articles.summary: The European Union's Cybersecurity Service (CERT-EU) has attributed the European Commission cloud hack to the TeamPCP threat group, saying the resulting breach exposed the data of at least 29 other Union entities. [...]

articles.title: Claude Code leak used to push infostealer malware on GitHub
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting the recent Claude Code source code leak by using fake GitHub repositories to deliver Vidar information-stealing malware. [...]

articles.title: Drift loses $280 million as North Korean hackers seize Security Council powers
articles.summary: The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]

articles.title: Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak
articles.summary: Within days of each other, Anthropic first leaked the source code to Claude Code, and then a critical vulnerability was found by Adversa AI. The post Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices
articles.summary: The DarkSword exploit kit has been used by both state-sponsored hackers and commercial spyware vendors. The post Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Residential proxies evaded IP reputation checks in 78% of 4B sessions
articles.summary: Researchers warn that residential proxies used to route malicious traffic are a big problem for IP reputation systems, as there is no clear distinction between attackers and legitimate users. [...]

articles.summary: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and gain access to the system with elevated privileges. articles.summary: From its GitHub repo: "Vite (French word for "quick", pronounced /vi?t/, like "veet") is a new breed of frontend build tooling that significantly improves the frontend development experience" [https://github.com/vitejs/vite].

articles.title: Adversaries Exploit Vacant Homes to Intercept Mail in Hybrid Cybercrime
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting vacant homes as "drop addresses" to intercept mail and enable fraud. Flare shows how postal services and fake identities are abused to turn mail into a fraud vector. [...]

articles.title: New Progress ShareFile flaws can be chained in pre-auth RCE attacks
articles.summary: Two vulnerabilities in Progress ShareFile, an enterprise-grade secure file transfer solution, can be chained to enable unauthenticated file exfiltration from affected environments. [...]
